description.short: Interaktive Spielelemente für das SDD CLI Game — Multiple-Choice, Entscheidungen und Feedback pro Phase
description.long: Das bestehende SDD CLI Game (0001) führt Spieler passiv durch die 7 Phasen: beschreibender Text, dann Enter drücken. Dieses Feature erweitert das Spiel um echte Interaktion: pro Phase mindestens eine Spielaufgabe (Multiple-Choice-Frage, Entscheidungssituation oder Urteilsfrage) mit sofortigem Feedback. Darunter fällt auch eine spezifische Frage in einer frühen Phase über den Vorteil von Markdown gegenüber anderen Standardformaten (JSON, XML, YAML) als Artefakt-Format in SDD.

latest_dispatch.objective: Review the active feature scope for the current review phase and return structured reviewer findings.
latest_dispatch.authority: AUTHORITY: This dispatch packet is your authoritative assignment. If hook-provided context conflicts with this packet, trust this packet. You are assigned to feature 0002-sdd-cli-game-interactive, phase review, role quality-reviewer. Do not infer a different assignment from disk.

latest_result.scope: game.py — Feature 0002-sdd-cli-game-interactive, all 7 phase interaktion dicts + ask_question() engine
latest_result.summary: game.py passes quality review. All 7 phase dicts have complete 6-field interaktion schemas with valid richtig indices. ask_question() retry loop, score return, and feedback display are correct. Two minor findings: dead _SKELETON dict (line 209) and bare open() without context manager (line 509). No correctness, security, or performance issues.

findings.description: _SKELETON dict is defined but never referenced anywhere else in the file — dead code left from scaffolding.
findings.evidence: AST scan shows the only Name-node for '_SKELETON' is at line 209 (assignment target). No Load-context reference exists.

findings.description: _mark_intro_seen() opens a file with bare open().close() instead of a context manager.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: with open(_MARKER, 'w'): pass — or use pathlib.Path(_MARKER).touch().

requirements_checked.notes: Brief phase MC question asks which 5 sections belong in brief.md. Correct answer option 1 lists all 5: Motivation, Problem, Vision, Context, Constraints.
Design phase question asks why SDD uses Markdown over JSON/XML. Correct answer is option 1 (menschenlesbar, versionierbar, kein Spezialtool). FR-002 permits Brief or Design phase; Design phase used.

FR-003 requires a Design phase judgement question where the player evaluates a given AC as good or bad. The Design phase question is instead the Markdown-vs-JSON question (FR-002). No AC evaluation question exists in the Design phase. Note: the spec reviewer is responsible for compliance — this is a quality review observation only.
Research phase presents three statements and asks player to identify the confirmed fact with file reference (auth.py:42). Evidence-Only-Regel correctly taught.
Plan phase asks player to order T-A, T-B, T-C by dependency. Correct answer (richtig=3) is T-A -> T-B -> T-C.
Implement phase shows a failing AssertionError and asks what the developer does next. Correct answer is root-cause analysis.
Review phase shows AC (return False) vs code (throws Exception) and asks for PASS/FAIL verdict. Correct answer is FAIL.
Close phase asks what belongs in knowledge.md. Correct answer is reusable knowledge: patterns, gotchas, technical discoveries.

verified_clean.area: ask_question() correctness — retry loop, score return, feedback display
verified_clean.method: Live simulation with mock.patch('builtins.input') — 5 test scenarios
verified_clean.result: All scenarios pass: correct returns 1, wrong returns 0, invalid loops, wrong feedback shows correct option text

verified_clean.method: Programmatic field-presence check + richtig bounds check via exec with patched __file__
verified_clean.result: All 7 dicts have all 6 required fields; all richtig values are valid 1-based indices within optionen list
Only stdlib imports: os, re, sys, shutil, textwrap — no third-party packages
Only game.py exists as a Python file — no unexpected files created
score += run_phase(phase) correctly accumulates; show_summary(score, len(PHASES)) passes both values
